body.title: [sprint] A max_tokens truncation is classified as a CAPABILITY failure and spends up a tier
body.description:
FOUND 2026-07-09 while building the escalation eval instrument. The same defect exists in BOTH the instrument and the domain; the instrument's copy is fixed (escalation_matrix.py TRUNC verdict, commit c378056e), the DOMAIN's copy is NOT.

The escalation walk (BaseDomain.run) has exactly three classes: done / cost / availability / capability. There is no class for 'the reply was cut off before the model finished'. A reasoning model (deepseek-r1:*) spends its budget inside <think>…</think>; when max_tokens runs out mid-thought, the reply contains an unclosed <think> and NO answer. GeneralDomain._run_attempt then runs answer_check on it, the check fails, _classify() returns 'capability', and the walk BUMPS A RUNG — spending up to a pricier tier because of a token budget.

GeneralDomain defaults max_tokens=1024, which is well inside the range where a deepseek-r1 reply truncates mid-<think>. So the default configuration reaches this bug.

WHY IT MATTERS BEYOND MONEY: a tier bump does not fix truncation. The stronger model is also a reasoning model, also thinks long, and will also truncate at the same budget — so the walk climbs the whole ladder and raises `inference-capability-ceiling`, an alarm that names the wrong cause. This is the availability-vs-capability confusion in a third flavour: 'Hex-DOWN is not a branch', and neither is 'the budget ran out'.

A truncation is a BUDGET failure. Bumping the tier is wrong (a bigger model thinks longer). Halting is wrong (the model may well be capable). The honest move is to retry at the SAME rung with a larger budget, bounded, exactly as availability re-selects at the same rung — and only then, if it still cannot answer within a sane budget, classify capability.

DETECTION (already implemented in escalation_matrix._unclosed_think + finish_reason check):
  finish_reason == 'length'  OR  '<think' in reply and '</think' not in reply

WORK:
- Add a BUDGET class to the walk's _classify (a 4th LoopResult outcome, e.g. LOOP_TRUNCATED), distinct from capability and availability.
- Bounded same-rung retry with a raised budget (mirror max_availability_retries — small and hard; a model that cannot answer inside a sane ceiling is a capability failure and only THEN bumps).
- Raise GeneralDomain's default max_tokens above the truncation floor for reasoning models, or make it derive from the routed model.
- The capability-ceiling alarm must never fire for a run whose every rung truncated — that alarm would name the wrong cause (CP3).

**Test plan:** red->green, hermetic (stub device, no live InferenceDevice). A stub returning finish_reason='length' with an unclosed <think> at hop 0 must NOT dispatch at hop 1; it must re-dispatch at hop 0 with a larger max_tokens. A stub that truncates at every budget must halt naming truncation, not capability-ceiling.

**Decision:** decision:D-domains-general-with-device-owned-specializations-2026-07-08

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
